steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The sample estimate is 31.6 oz and the margin of error is 0.04 oz. The margin of error creates an interval around the estimated mean, not around individual backpack weights.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Lower bound: 31.6 - 0.04 = 31.56 oz. Upper bound: 31.6 + 0.04 = 31.64 oz. The interval [31.56, 31.64] oz is the plausible range for the true population mean.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
• A: The mean weight of all backpacks is between 31.56 oz and 31.64 oz — This correctly applies the margin of error to the estimated mean. A is correct.
  • B: The actual weights of all backpacks are between 31.56 oz and 31.64 oz — Margin of error describes uncertainty about the mean, not the spread of individual weights. Eliminated.
  • C: The actual weights of all backpacks from the sample are between 31.56 oz and 31.64 oz — Same error as B; margin of error is not a bound on individual observations. Eliminated.
  • D: The mean weight is exactly 31.6 oz — This ignores the margin of error and treats the estimate as certain. Eliminated.
